content.full_text:
Re:
Farn PourrcAI- PRACTTcES CouvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
March 25,2005
Charlene Cruz
County of Los Angeles
Registrar-Recorder/County Clerk
Campaign Finance
Disclosure Section, Room 2003
12400Imperial Highway
Norwalk, CA 90650
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our FiIe No. I-05-046
Dear Ms. Craz:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Politicat Reform Act (the "Act').r Since your inquiry is general in
nature, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.2
QUESTTON
How long must your agency retain duplicate copies of campaign statements?
CONCLUSION
Your agency is not required to retain more than one copy of a report or statement.
FACTS
The Registrar-Recorder of Los Angeles County is the filing officer for certain
campaign statements. I confirmed with you in a telephone conversation that you are
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appeffat Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not p.oirid" the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 831 l4; regulation 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.) 
-
tua6.id


File No. I-05-046
Page No. 2
inquiring about the copies your agency receives under sections 8a215(a)(2) and,
8421s(b)Q).
ANALYSIS
Govemment Code section 84215 addresses where campaign reports and
statements must be filed. Section842l5(a)Q) requires statewide elected officers and
candidates for these offices other than the Board of Equalization, supreme court justices,
and their controlled committees, committees formed or existing primarily to support or
oppose these candidates, elected officers, supreme court justices, or statewide measures,
or the qualification of state ballot measures, and all state general purpose committees,
among others, to file two copies of their campaign statements with you. In addition,
section 84215@)Q) requires members of the Legislature or Board of Equalization, court
of appeal justices, superior court judges, candidates for those offices, their controlled
committees, and committees formed or existing primarily to support or oppose these
candidates or officeholders to file two copies with you if Los Angeles County is the
county with the largest number of registered voters in their districts.
Section 81009 addresses retention periods for reports and statements. It provides:
"...(D Copies of reports or statements shall be retained by the
officer with whom they are filed for a period of not less than four years,
provided, however, that a filing officer is not required to retain more
than one copy of areport of statement...." (Emphasis added.)
Therefore, although certain filers must provide your agency with two copies of
campaign statements, based on the literal reading of this section, your agency is only
required to keep one of the copies.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
hr.it^*
By: Trish Mayer Ar)
Political Reform Consultant

QUESTION: How long must your agency retain duplicate copies of campaign statements?

CONCLUSION: Your agency is not required to retain more than one copy of a report or statement.
